courseDetails.fullDescription: ITIL 4 Foundation is the latest evolution of the most widely adopted guidance for ITSM. Its audience ranges from IT and business students taking their first steps in service management to seasoned professionals familiar with earlier versions of ITIL and other sources of industry best practice. ITIL certification is an essential requirement for professionals who need to learn the fundamentals of ITIL frameworks and how they may be used to enhance the quality of IT service management.
courseDetails.targetAudience: IT professionals engaged in Service Delivery, IT Managers/Support Teams, System Administrators/Analysts, Operations Managers, Database Administrators, Service Delivery Professionals
keyConcepts: IT service management in the modern world
The ITIL Service Value system or SVS
The four dimensions model
Key concepts of service management such as Value and value co-creation, stakeholders, Service consumers and stakeholders, Costs, Risks, Utility and warranty
Guiding Principles, and the Service Value Chain along with the continuous Improvement Model
ITIL management practices such as General management practices, Service management practices and technical management practices
learningOutcomes: Apply ITIL guiding principles in practice
Understand the ITIL service value chain
Know how to improve service management practices
Understand the four dimensions of service management
benefitsOrganizations: Improved service quality and customer satisfaction
Better alignment between IT and business objectives
Reduced costs through efficient service management
prerequisites: None - suitable for beginners in ITSM
courseDetails.instructorBio: 15+ years of experience in IT Service Management. ITIL Expert certified with extensive experience training over 3000 professionals across multiple countries. Specializes in ITSM implementation and service improvement.

courseDetails.fullDescription: DevOps is a software development methodology that combines software development (Dev) with information technology operations (Ops). The goal of DevOps is to shorten the systems development life cycle while delivering software releases frequently in close alignment with business objectives in a faster, better, and cheaper fashion. The DevOps approach to work covers three critical success factors: cultural shift, improvement of practices and processes, and leverage of automation technologies.
courseDetails.targetAudience: IT practitioners, DevOps professionals, Agile practitioners, Project managers, Software developers, Operations staff who want to understand DevOps principles
keyConcepts: DevOps concepts and terminology
Core DevOps Principles
Key DevOps Practices
Business and Technology Frameworks
Culture, Behaviors and Operating Models
Automation and Architecting DevOps Toolchains
Measurement, Metrics, and Reporting
Sharing, Shadowing and Evolving
learningOutcomes: Benefits to the business and IT
Continuous Integration, Continuous Delivery, testing, security and the Three Ways
DevOps relationship to Agile, Lean and ITSM
Improved workflows, communication, and feedback loops
Automation practices including deployment pipelines and DevOps toolchains
benefitsOrganizations: Reduced deployment failures, rollbacks, and time to recover
benefitsIndividuals: Knowledge of automation tools and practices
courseDetails.instructorBio: DevOps practitioner with 10+ years of experience in software development and operations. Has led digital transformations at Fortune 500 companies. Certified DevOps Leader and trainer with expertise in CI/CD, automation, and cloud technologies.

courseDetails.fullDescription: PRINCE2 is a project management method that offers a structured, scalable, and tailorable approach to project management. Its focus on governance, risk management, and benefits realization sets it apart from other qualifications. By integrating agile concepts and emphasizing tailoring, the new PRINCE2 is even more adaptable to diverse project environments.
courseDetails.targetAudience: Project managers, aspiring project managers, team members, project board members, anyone involved in managing or working on projects
keyConcepts: Central Focus on People Management, recognizing the importance of human capital in project success
Enhanced Flexibility and Customization, adapting to the unique requirements of each project, across sectors
Embracing Digital and Data Management, equipping you with the right tools to keep your projects up to date in the digital era
Integration of Sustainability into Project Performance, aligning projects with environmental and social responsibility goals
Compatibility with Agile, Lean, and ITIL, enhancing overall project performance
Simplified language and content accessible to all professionals
learningOutcomes: Understand the PRINCE2 methodology and principles
Apply PRINCE2 themes in project context
Understand roles and responsibilities in PRINCE2
benefitsOrganizations: Standardized project management approach across organization
benefitsIndividuals: Enhanced career prospects in project management
prerequisites: None - suitable for beginners in project management
courseDetails.instructorBio: Certified PRINCE2 Practitioner and trainer with 12+ years of project management experience. Has successfully delivered projects across various industries including IT, construction, and finance. Expert in project governance and tailoring methodologies to organizational needs.
